chapter_title.ar: بَابُ الْمُحْرِمِ يَتَزَوَّجُ أَوْ يُزَوِّجُ وَ يُطَلِّقُ وَ يَشْتَرِي الْجَوَارِيَ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 102 – The one in Ihraam gets married, or marries and divorces, and he buys the slave girl

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/345)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/345)
text: الْمُحْرِمُ لَا يَتَزَوَّجُ فَإِنْ فَعَلَ فَنِكَاحُهُ بَاطِلٌ.

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Safwan, from Muawiya Bin Ammar who said,
‘The one in Ihraam cannot get married. So if he were to do so, then his marriage would be invalid’.
en.sarwar: 4. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from Safwan from Mu‘awiyah ibn ‘Ammar who has said the following: “He (the Imam), ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘A person in the state of Ihram cannot marry, if one does so one’s marriage is invalid.’”
